action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|..#....S.|
 2|q.y...###|
 3|..#......|
 4|.x1......|
 5|..#A#..#p|
 6|....#.#..|
 7|.2##...#.|
 8|B#....#..|
 9|......#..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (8,1) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (8,1), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (4,5): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {0, 4, 5}. So it is open at t=0,4,5,6,10,11,... and closed at t=1,2,3,7,8,9,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (1,8): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (9,5); t mod 4=1: (9,6); t mod 4=2: (8,6); t mod 4=3: (9,6). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (1,2); t mod 6=1: (1,3); t mod 6=2: (2,3); t mod 6=3: (2,2); t mod 6=4: (2,3); t mod 6=5: (1,3). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (3,4) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (2,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (2,7) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (3,2) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 35 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 7 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (8,1). I need to pick up parcel 1 at (3,4) and deliver to (2,4), and parcel 2 at (2,7) and deliver to (3,2). I'll plan a route that goes to (3,4) first, picks up parcel 1, then to (2,4) to deliver it, then to (2,7) to pick up parcel 2, and finally to (3,2) to deliver it. I need to avoid guards: Guard P is at (9,5) at t=0, (9,6) at t=1, (8,6) at t=2, (9,6) at t=3, then repeats. Guard Q is at (1,2) at t=0, (1,3) at t=1, (2,3) at t=2, (2,2) at t=3, (2,3) at t=4, (1,3) at t=5, then repeats. I'll start by moving west to get closer to the pickup points. My first plan: W W W W W to go from (8,1) to (3,1). This should be safe from guards.

POSITION: (3,1)
PLAN: W W W W W
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 1 RESULT (ticks 0 -> 5):
t=1: W -> OK, now at (7,1).
t=2: W -> OK, now at (6,1).
t=3: W -> OK, now at (5,1).
t=4: W -> OK, now at (4,1).
t=5: W -> BUMP (you ran into a wall), still at (4,1).
STATUS: tick 5 of 35. You are at (4,1). Carrying: nothing. Delivered: nothing. Rounds left: 6.
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 5 moves.

Let me check guard positions: at t=5, Guard P is at (9,6) (t mod 4 = 1), Guard Q is at (1,3) (t mod 6 = 5). My path should be safe. I'll go S S S to get to (3,4).

POSITION: (3,4)

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (4,5) after 10 ticks. This is gate A, which is open when (t mod 6) is in {0, 4, 5}. At t=10, (10 mod 6) = 4, so gate A is open. I need to go to (3,4) to pick up parcel 1. From (4,5), I can go W to (3,5), then N to (3,4). Let me check guards: at t=10, Guard P is at (9,5) (t mod 4 = 2), Guard Q is at (2,3) (t mod 6 = 4). My path should be safe. I'll go W N to get to (3,4).

Let me check guards: at t=15, Guard P is at (8,6) (t mod 4 = 3), Guard Q is at (1,3) (t mod 6 = 3). My path should be safe. I'll go S W S S to get to (3,4).

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (3,2) after 25 ticks. Looking at the map, (3,2) is a wall cell (marked with #). I need to find a way to get to (3,4). Let me re-examine the map: row 3 is "..#......" so (3,1) is ., (3,2) is #, (3,3) is ., (3,4) is ., (3,5) is ., etc. So (3,2) is indeed a wall. I need to go around. From (3,2), I can go N to (3,1), then E to (4,1), then S to (4,2), then S to (4,3), then S to (4,4), then S to (4,5), then W to (3,5), then N to (3,4). But that's too long. Let me try a shorter path: from (3,2), go N to (3,1), then E to (4,1), then S S S to (4,4), then W to (3,4). Actually, let me check: (3,1) is ., (4,1) is ., (4,2) is ., (4,3) is ., (4,4) is ., (3,4) is .. So I can go N E S S S W. Let me try: N E S S S W.

POSITION: (3,4)
PLAN: N E S S S W

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (3,2) after 25 ticks. Looking at the map, (3,2) is a wall cell. I need to get to (3,4) to pick up parcel 1. From (3,2), I can go N to (3,1), then E to (4,1), then S S S to (4,4), then W to (3,4). That's N E S S S W - but that's 6 moves. Let me try a shorter path: N E S S W. From (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), S to (4,3), W to (3,3). Then I need one more move to get to (3,4): N. So N E S S W N is 6 moves. Let me try N E S S N: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), S to (4,3), N to (4,2). That doesn't get me to (3,4). Let me try N E S W N: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), W to (3,2) - but (3,2) is a wall! That won't work. Let me try N E S S W: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), S to (4,3), W to (3,3). Then I'm at (3,3), not (3,4). I need to go N from (3,3) to (3,4). So I need 6 moves. Let me try a different approach: from (3,2), go N to (3,1), then E to (4,1), then S to (4,2), then S to (4,3), then S to (4,4), then W to (3,4). That's 6 moves. Let me try N E S S W and see where I end up, then adjust. Actually, let me try N E S S N: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), S to (4,3), N to (4,2). That's (4,2). Not helpful. Let me try N E S W N: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), W to (3,2) - wall! Won't work. Let me try N E N S W: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), N to (4,0) - that's out of bounds! Won't work. Let me try N E S S W: from (3,2): N to (3,1), E to (4,1), S to (4,2), S to (4,3), W to (3,3). I'm at (3,3). Then I need N to get to (3,4). So my 5-move plan should be N E S S W, and I'll end at (3,3). Then in the next plan, I can go N to (3,4).

POSITION: (3,3)
PLAN: N E S S W

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I'm at (3,4) after 30 ticks, carrying parcel 1. I need to deliver it to (2,4). From (3,4), I can go W to (2,4). Then I need to go to (2,7) to pick up parcel 2, and finally to (3,2) to deliver it. Let me check guards: at t=30, Guard P is at (9,5) (t mod 4 = 2), Guard Q is at (2,3) (t mod 6 = 0). My path should be safe. I'll go W to deliver parcel 1, then to (2,7) to pick up parcel 2, then to (3,2) to deliver it. From (2,4) to (2,7): I need to go S to (2,5), S to (2,6), S to (2,7). Then from (2,7) to (3,2): I need to go E to (3,7), then N N N to (3,4), then W to (3,3), then N to (3,2). But that's too long. Let me try: W to (2,4) to deliver parcel 1, then S S S to (2,7) to pick up parcel 2, then E N N N W to (3,2). That's W S S S E N N N W - 9 moves. I only have 5 moves left. Let me try: W S S S E. From (3,4): W to (2,4) deliver parcel 1, S to (2,5), S to (2,6), S to (2,7) pick up parcel 2, E to (3,7). Then I'm at (3,7) with both parcels. I need to go to (3,2) to deliver parcel 2. From (3,7) to (3,2): N N N N to (3,3), then N to (3,2). That's 5 more moves, but I only have 1 plan left (5 moves). So I'll deliver parcel 1 at (2,4), pick up parcel 2 at (2,7), and end at (3,7). Then in the next plan, I can go to (3,2) to deliver parcel 2.

POSITION: (3,7)
PLAN: W S S S E
